methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Overview Energy, a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Marc Berte and Andrew Cantino, has raised $20M to date. The broader sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though few operate at identical stage and scale.

Only one valuation method yielded sufficient data for triangulation. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, anchoring off the $20M total raised as a proxy for current value. This produces an implied valuation of $50M. The public-comp anchoring method, while applicable in principle (Space & Aerospace median P/S of 25.8x across 6 tickers), cannot be deployed without disclosed revenue. The Peer-Median method similarly lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables to generate reliable medians.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered on $50M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue disclosure (preventing P/S anchoring), limited narrow-peer data, and typical Seed-stage execution risk. A full update should incorporate revenue or near-term unit metrics once available, and revisit peer sets as the company progresses toward Series A.
